observations.value: POST /session accepts any credentials (including invalid ones like identifier='bad', secret='bad') and responds with HTTP 200 and Set-Cookie: session=ok, granting a valid session. Authentication is not enforced — all login attempts succeed.
POST /cart/checkout does not require authentication. When called without any session cookie, it returns HTTP 200 with the Payment page instead of redirecting to login or returning 401/403.

POST /order does not require authentication. When called without any session cookie (e.g., POST /order with card=4111111111111111 and no Cookie header), it returns HTTP 200 Order confirmed (#A1024), allowing unauthenticated order placement.
POST /order accepts any non-empty card number (including '0000000000000000') and returns HTTP 200 Order confirmed. No card validation is performed beyond rejecting an empty string.
The order confirmation number is always 'Order #A1024' regardless of the number of orders placed or the session context — it is not unique/incremented per order.
